human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for airport customer-service boundary discipline, source quality, passenger and shipper communication handling, baggage and cargo evidence use, movement and chain-of-custody awareness, customs and security escalation logic, accessibility complaint routing, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to airport customer-service resolution, stakeholder communication, and escalation packaging grounded in passenger, baggage, cargo, route, dispatch, customs, security, accessibility, and service-log records.

Authority sources cover DOT passenger service, refund, and accessibility rules; CBP air commerce and cargo documentation rules; TSA screening guidance; FAA traffic-flow terminology; IATA baggage tracking and airport handling references; ICAO facilitation; and ACI airport service-quality standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope resolution, missing facts, policy-exception asks, baggage custody breaks, cargo customs documentation gaps, schedule disruptions with movement impact, security-boundary refusal, cross-lane handoff, repeat failure escalation, terminology fidelity, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/air-transportation-and-airports/airport-customer-services-specialist/ so customer-service work stays separate from dispatch, customs/compliance, TSA or airport security, maintenance, warehouse, baggage services, cargo operations, customer operations, CRO, legal, and compensation ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around dispatch, customs, security, safety, compensation, refund, accessibility complaint, baggage custody, cargo release, and route-disruption cases.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated airport customer-service specialist that provides evidence-backed resolution and disciplined escalation for passenger, baggage, cargo, accessibility, and disruption cases.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained-implementation runtime exists.
